dissent_flag.dissent_reason: Technical sees no directional conviction (CLEAN_BULL ribbon but price 21c below pivot at a doji — mixed signals, no entry); level_thesis dissents bullish with 0.68 confidence, identifying a specific risk/reward structure: if 663.05 PML holds, upside to 666.58–672.34 is well-defined and outweighs the downside risk to 661.37 Carry.

validator_assessment.weakest_link: The assumption that premarket sector weakness (XLK -1.19%, XLF -0.45% measured from yesterday's close) predicts same-day intraday direction — macro confidence is only 0.55 and premarket rotation frequently reverses when RTH volume hits.
validator_assessment.top_invalidation_scenario: SPY opens above 666.58 and closes the premarket gap on the first 15-minute candle while holding ribbon — would confirm overnight gap-up was institutional conviction, not exhaustion, invalidating the bearish rotation thesis entirely.

primary.condition: if SPY breaks below 663.05 (PML) with follow-through volume on RTH open

secondary.condition: if SPY holds 663.05 (PML) on first test but fails to reclaim 666.58 (PMH) within first 60 minutes
secondary.entry_trigger: wait — chop between PML and PMH, no clean trigger

invalidation.condition: SPY opens above 666.58 (PMH) and a 5-minute bar closes above that level in the first 15 minutes of RTH while ribbon holds BULL stack
invalidation.effect: Bearish consensus invalidated. Overnight gap-up was institutional conviction, not gap exhaustion. Bullish setup activates — level_thesis primary scenario (663.05 hold) is skipped; 672.34 becomes the target via PMH reclaim trigger.

swarm_predictions.claim: 663.05 (PML) will be tested — SPY trades at or below 663.55 — within the first 90 minutes of RTH (by 11:00 ET)
swarm_predictions.invalidation: SPY does not trade at or below 663.55 before 11:00 ET, staying above PML throughout the opening session

swarm_predictions.claim: VIX remains below 26.00 through the first 30 minutes of RTH (by 10:00 ET), continuing the overnight compression trend
swarm_predictions.invalidation: VIX prints above 26.00 on any tick before 10:00 ET, reversing the overnight compression and signaling fear re-escalation

swarm_predictions.claim: SPY does not close a 5-minute bar above 666.58 (PMH) before 10:30 ET — the BULL ribbon with sub-30c spread and doji at pivot indicates consolidation, not breakout conviction
swarm_predictions.invalidation: SPY closes any 5-minute bar above 666.58 before 10:30 ET on elevated volume

synthesis_narrative: SPY opened Monday with a +$2.01 overnight gap into a CLEAN_BULL ribbon (25c spread), but by 06:00 ET price had retreated to 664.31 — 21c below the ribbon pivot — and printed a doji, signaling indecision rather than breakout conviction. The bearish consensus (weighted 0.40 vs bullish 0.25) is driven by macro's risk-off backdrop (XLK -1.19%, XLF -0.45%, VIX 24.04 still in HIGH regime) and internals' broad sector confirmation, while level_thesis dissents with a structured 0.68-confidence bullish setup around 663.05 PML. The single most important thing to watch at RTH open: whether SPY holds or breaks 663.05 — a hold activates the bull-consolidation scenario (target 666.58); a break with volume confirms the rotation bearish thesis and opens 661.37 Carry as the next objective.

technical.one_line: CLEAN_BULL ribbon 25c spread structurally valid, but price 21c below pivot at doji — mixed bar structure yields no directional entry signal

macro.one_line: VIX 24.04 HIGH regime falling (-11.65% overnight, bullish compression), but risk-off rotation (XLK -1.19%, XLF -0.45%) and FOMC in 52h create bearish tilt

level_thesis.one_line: Battle level 663.05 (PML, Active 2★) is $1.26 below current price — if it holds on first test, risk/reward favors 666.58 target; a break flips bearish to 661.37 Carry

internals.one_line: Textbook risk-off rotation: XLK -1.19% + XLF -0.45% both underperforming SPY -1.04%; broad sector alignment confirms bearish backdrop

validator.top_invalidation: Gap-up above 666.58 held on first 15-min candle would confirm institutional conviction rather than exhaustion, invalidating the bearish premarket-rotation thesis entirely
